rulings.Case Title: CARMEN VALDEZ-MADRANOS VS. MERIDIAN MANAGEMENT GROUP, A BUSINESS ENTITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT

rulings.Case Title: ALISON PAIGE GOODMAN VS. ARMANDO RODRIGUEZ et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 31, 2013, line 5 - DEFENDANT ARMANDO RODRIGUEZ's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. "3 Day Notice" is not compliant with CCP 1161(2) and cannot support a cause of action for unlawful detainer. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JCSF INVESTMENTS, LP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. LUCIO RODRIGUEZ et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 31, 2013, line 6 - DEFENDANT SOFIA VERGARA's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant Vergara's Demurer to Complaint is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Unlawful Detainer pursuant to CCP 1161a is sufficiently alleged. Demurrer improperly raises issues of fact as to pre-judgment claimant's status as tenants. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: VICTOR TUNG et al VS. AMY QI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 31, 2013, line 8 - Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction. Temporary Restraining Order and Order to Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction is GRANTED as follows: Amy Qi is enjoined from entering the premises located at 716 Monterey Blvd. (ground floor), San Francisco California and from removing the personal property of Plaintiff from the premises pending a determination or final resolution of this case. Non-opposition filed. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: THEODOR V HEYERMANN VS. LAURA D MILLER et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, July 31, 2013, line 9 - DEFENDANT LAURA MILLER's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Defendant's Notice of Demurrer fails to comply with CRC 3.1110. The Demurrer and Points and Authorities in support of Demurrer disclose two separate grounds for demurrer to complaint for unlawful detainer. Taking allegations in complaint as true, the Court has jurisdiction over this Limited case and Plaintiff's recovery shall not exceed $25,000, unless Plaintiff moves and the Court grants motion to re-classify. Also, Defendant's demurrer does not address any deficiencies in the second cause of action. Defendant does not separately demur to the first cause of action. CRC 3.1112 (4). (See Demurrer 1:20-23). Therefore, pleading for unlawful detainer states facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action, even if the first cause of action is defective. =(501/REQ)
